mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals broad economic output expansion, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors price in the recovery.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism yields negative returns in more than 55% of observations when INDPRO is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by at least 0.5 percentage points over three months indicates tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer income and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: After trigger activation, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average return following triggers is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and low level of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal a housing market recovery supported by accommodative monetary policy, which stimulates economic activity and boosts equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST * FEDFUNDS) in a linear regression of 20-day forward returns has a t-statistic below 1.96 or a coefficient with inconsistent sign across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime (FEDFUNDS near zero), rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real yields and may signal monetary policy lagging the cycle, which pressures equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the correlation between CPIAUCSL inflation and 20-day forward returns is positive or insignificant (p > 0.10), or the average return during such regimes with rising inflation is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly increase in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) by more than 1% month-over-month signals a surge in consumer spending, boosting near-term earnings expectations and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, 5-day forward returns are negative in more than half of the observations, or the average excess return over baseline is less than 0.1% per event.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product (GDPC1) reflects durable aggregate demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue and profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as the economy strengthens.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 or negative over any 5-year window, or the strategy of buying after positive GDP reports yields negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is rising and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, the joint condition of expanding output with accommodative monetary policy amplifies the positive signal for equity markets, leading to higher S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with output growth
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient (INDPRO * FEDFUNDS) in a regression of 20-day returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or has a negative sign inconsistent with the hypothesis across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Short-term declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) immediately improve consumer sentiment and reduce economic uncertainty, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as risk appetite increases.
failure_condition: The correlation between week-over-week changes in UNRATE (monthly revisions considered) and 5-day forward returns is not statistically negative (p > 0.05) or the average return following UNRATE drops is not positive.
